# reorient { #neuroim.reorient }

```python
reorient(x, orient)
```

Remap the grid-to-world coordinates mapping of an image.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                                                                                       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Default    |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| x      | [NeuroSpace](`neuroim.neuro_space.NeuroSpace`) or [NeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.NeuroVol`) | The object to reorient                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | _required_ |
| orient | str or list of str                                                                         | The orientation code indicating the remapped axes. Can be a 3-letter string like "RAS" or list like ["R", "A", "S"] Each position represents: - First: "R" (Right) or "L" (Left) - Second: "A" (Anterior) or "P" (Posterior) - Third: "S" (Superior) or "I" (Inferior) | _required_ |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                       | Description               |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------|
|        | [NeuroSpace](`neuroim.neuro_space.NeuroSpace`) or [NeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.NeuroVol`) | A reoriented version of x |

## R Equivalent {.doc-section .doc-section-r-equivalent}

neuroim2::reorient

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> # Reorient a space to RAS
>>> space_ras = reorient(space, "RAS")
>>>
>>> # Or using a list
>>> space_ras = reorient(space, ["R", "A", "S"])
```
